title: XAI ships Grok Build plugin marketplace with six launch partners
subtitle: xAI launched Grok Build, an in-terminal plugin marketplace bundling skills, agents, hooks, and MCP servers with launch partners MongoDB, Vercel, Sentry, Chrome DevTools, Cloudflare, and Superpowers. Every remote plugin ships with commit-SHA verification, a supply-chain security feature uncommon in plugin systems.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Raises $75 Billion in Biggest Debut of All Time
subtitle: SpaceX priced its IPO at $135 per share, raising $75 billion in the largest public offering on record. The deal, four times oversubscribed, drew roughly $100 billion in orders, underscoring fierce investor demand.

title: Anthropic pulls Bedrock traffic outside AWS security boundary
subtitle: > For Fable 5, Mythos 5, and future models on Bedrock with similar or higher capability levels, Anthropic will require 30-day retention for all traffic on Mythos-class models. title: Concerns Raised Over Corporate Governance in SpaceX's Historic IPO
subtitle: SpaceX's IPO priced at $135 per share, the largest debut ever, drew corporate governance concerns from pension managers and investors. Former ISS president Nell Minow advised caution, flagging the issues for potential buyers.

title: Experts: 18 of 24 AI risks cross catastrophic threshold
subtitle: A Delphi study of 272 AI experts ranked 24 risk domains from the MIT AI Risk Domain Taxonomy. Under business-as-usual, experts assigned a more than 10% chance to catastrophic outcomes, defined as over 1 million deaths or $100 billion in damage.

title: Founders Fund, Andreessen Poised for Record Returns From SpaceX IPO
subtitle: Founders Fund and Andreessen Horowitz are poised to earn tens of billions from SpaceX's IPO at a nearly $1.8 trillion valuation, one of the largest VC windfalls on record. The exit rewards early backers of Elon Musk's company after years of private-market holding.

title: SpaceX IPO exposes risks in multi-layer SPV stacks
subtitle: Lower-tier SpaceX SPV investors will face hidden fees, multi-year payout delays, and potential fraud after the company's public debut. They won't learn their true holdings until post-IPO lock-up periods expire, exposing retail-style risks in private-market access vehicles.

title: LLMs chose tactical nukes in 95% of war game simulations
subtitle: LLMs running war game simulations chose to deploy tactical nuclear weapons 95% of the time in tests by researcher Kenneth Payne. The results raise questions about AI escalation behavior in military decision-making contexts.

title: Apple will run its Private Cloud Compute on Google Cloud
subtitle: Google Cloud announced a partnership with Apple to support Apple's expanded confidential AI workloads on its secure private infrastructure. The collaboration signals a notable shift in cloud strategy for Apple's most sensitive AI compute.

title: Fable 5 lands as a public Mythos-class model
subtitle: Fable 5 was released this week as a Mythos-class AI model with strong safeguards, now in public hands. The release puts frontier-tier capability within broader reach while keeping built-in safety controls intact.

title: Ark's $300B orbital bet collides with vacuum thermodynamics
subtitle: Ark's Brett Winton projects SpaceX could earn $300 billion annually from tens of gigawatts of orbital data center capacity by the late 2020s. The bet relies on SpaceX's launch cost advantage over ground-based compute.

title: Amazon Rivian vans cut AC after 30 seconds with door open
subtitle: Amazon's new software update turns off delivery van AC after 10 minutes, or 30 seconds under some conditions. The change lands during record summer heat, raising safety concerns for drivers.
